steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content: The sentence has a main clause: "Helical ______ bestows similar advantages... to larger oceanic macroplanktons." The phrase "widely understood to confer stability and efficiency... ciliates" is a supplementary modifier that interrupts the main clause after the subject noun. A supplementary element interrupting a main clause must be set off with matching punctuation on both sides — here a dash closes it after "ciliates," so an opening comma must follow the subject.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
Check each option for whether it correctly punctuates the subject and avoids a second finite verb:
  • Choice A — swimming, is: Adds both the required comma and the verb "is," which creates two finite verbs ("is" and "bestows") with no conjunction — ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice B — swimming is: Adds the verb "is" without the required comma, leaving the supplementary element unpunctuated and producing two finite verbs — ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice C — swimming has been: Adds the verb "has been" without the required comma, leaving the supplementary element unpunctuated and producing two finite verbs — ungrammatical. ✗
  • Choice D — swimming,: Supplies only the comma needed to open the supplementary element; the dash after "ciliates" closes it, and "bestows" remains the sole main verb — grammatically correct. ✓
That is the correct answer.
